prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
Spanish  
“Si no sembraste un árbol cuando eras joven, planta uno hoy sin excusas.”  
(The proverb keeps its urgency by stressing regret-free action in the present, not the literal 20-year gap.)

Japanese  
「木を植えるならいつが一番良いか？答えは二十年前、次に良いのは今である。」  
(The rhythm mirrors the original’s two-step punch—past regret, immediate remedy—while sounding natural in Japanese.)
